label: 400_weo_weo update_outlook weo_world economic
description: The International Monetary Fund's World Economic Outlook reports provided regular updates on global growth trends, particularly focusing on emerging and developing economies. These reports highlighted the challenges faced by advanced economies and the vulnerabilities in the euro area.

earliest_articles.title: IMF World Economic Outlook (WEO) Update -- A Policy-Driven, Multispeed Recovery, January 2010

earliest_articles.title: Global Economy: Continuing Recovery But Clouds on the Horizon
earliest_articles.excerpt: The macroeconomic forecasts in the IMF's latest World Economic Outlook update reflect two opposing forces. Looking back, say over the first half of the year, numbers about economic activity have come in strong, indeed somewhat stronger than we had forecast. These would give reasons to be more optimistic than we were…

earliest_articles.title: Two-speed Global Recovery Continues
earliest_articles.excerpt: (Version in Español | Français | Русский | عربي| 中文 | 日本語 ) The world economic recovery continues. But it remains a two-speed recovery: slow in advanced countries, and much faster in emerging and developing economies. As a result, tensions and risks are emerging, which require strong policy responses. The outlook For…

newest_articles.title: World Economic Outlook, October 2016: Subdued Demand: Symptoms and Remedies
newest_articles.excerpt: Summary According to the October 2016 "World Economic Outlook," global growth is projected to slow to 3.1 percent in 2016 before recovering to 3.4 percent in 2017. The forecast, revised down by 0.1 percentage point for 2016 and 2017 relative to April’s report, reflects a more subdued outlook for advanced economies…

newest_articles.title: World Economic Outlook, April 2016: Too Slow for Too Long
newest_articles.excerpt: Summary Major macroeconomic realignments are affecting prospects differentially across the world’s countries and regions. The April 2016 WEO examines the causes and implications of these realignments—including the slowdown and rebalancing in China, a further decline in commodity prices, a related slowdown in…
